message:
🌱 Add opentofu: -exclude flag for targeted plan/apply mission (#1513)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "opentofu-426-exclude-flag-for-targeted-plan-apply",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "opentofu: -exclude flag for targeted plan/apply",
+    "description": "-exclude flag for targeted plan/apply. Requested by 54+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current opentofu setup",
+        "description": "Verify your opentofu version and configuration:\n```bash\ntofu version\n```\nThis feature requires a working opentofu installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review opentofu configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant opentofu configuration:\nThis RFC details a new flag, `-exclude`, which would do the inverse of `-target` - that is, plan/apply all resources _except_ those specified by the exclude flag and their descendants."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for -exclude flag for targeted plan/apply",
+        "description": "> While I can appreciate these arguments, I don't support it. The user using the tool has better context of what they need, and a better understanding of their desire. I think we have smart users and we shouldn't complicate their lives by limiting what they can do. After all, why have -exclude in\n```yaml\n-exclude module.entire_environment \\\n  -include module.entire_environment.module.network \\\n  -include module.entire_environment.module.application \\\n  -exclude module.entire_environment.module.application.module.eks\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"-exclude flag for targeted plan/apply\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "> While I can appreciate these arguments, I don't support it. The user using the tool has better context of what they need, and a better understanding of their desire. I think we have smart users and we shouldn't complicate their lives by limiting what they can do.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "-exclude module.entire_environment \\\n  -include module.entire_environment.module.network \\\n  -include module.entire_environment.module.application \\\n  -exclude module.entire_environment.module.application.module.eks",
+        "opentf plan \\\n  -exclude module.entire_environment \\\n  -include module.entire_environment.module.network \\\n  -include module.entire_environment.module.application \\\n  -exclude module.entire_environment.module.application.module.eks",
+        "-exclude module.entire_environment \\\n  -target module.entire_environment.module.network \\\n  -target module.entire_environment.module.application \\\n  -exclude module.entire_environment.module.application.module.eks"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {

+      "opentofu",
+      "sandbox",
+      "app-definition",
+      "feature"
+    ],

+    ],

+    ],

+    },

+  },

+    ],

+  },
